sections.paragraph:
pret. טֹבוּ; (for the future, the form יִיטַב, from יָטַב is used.)

(1) to be good (Arab. طَلبَ Med. Ye, to be good, pleasant, agreeable, especially used of a pleasant smell [“Ch. Syr. id.”], compare בָּאַשׁ, בְּאֵשׁ). Only used impersonally in the following phrases-(a) טֹוב לִי it is good for me, it goes well with me, Deu 5:30 15:16 19:13 Num 11:18 also, to be well, to be in good health, 1Sa 16:16. Followed by אֶל id. 1Sa 20:12. Job 13:9, הֲטֹוב כִּי it is well for you that.-As to the passage Job 10:3, see letter b.-(b) טֹוב בְּעֵינַי it is good in my eyes, i.e. “it pleases me,” Num 24:1. In the later books it is followed by עַל 1Ch 13:2. Est 1:19, אִם־עַל־הַמֶּלֶךְ טֹוב “if it please the king.” Est 3:9 5:4 , 7:3 Neh 2:5 compare Ezr 5:17 once followed by לְ Job 10:3, הֲטֹוב לְךָ “does it please thee?” So Vulg. Ch.

(2) to be beautiful, pleasant, Num 24:5 Son 4:10.

(3) to be cheerful, merry (a common meaning in Syriac), used especially of the heart, 1Sa 25:36 2Sa 13:28 Est 1:10.

Hiphil הֵטִיב-

(1) to do well, to do something rightly, with an acc. 1Ki 8:18 2Ki 10:30.

(2) to do good to some one, to confer benefits, Eze 36:11.

(3) to make fair, to adorn, Hos 10:1.

(4) to make cheerful, Ecc 11:9. הֵיטִיב, from יָטַב, is more commonly used.

[Derivatives, the following words, and pr.n. טָֽבְאֵל, and טַבְרִמֹּון.]

טוב

f. טֹובָה adj.-

(1) good, in various senses-(a) physically, as a good (i.e. fertile) land, Exo 3:8 a good tree, 2Ki 3:19 good gold, i.e. pure, Gen 2:12.

(b) ἠθικῶς, good, kind, upright, Isa 5:20 and neutr. goodness, uprightness, kindness. עָשָׂה טֹוב he acted well, he lived honestly, Psa 34:15 37:3 Ecc 7:20. עָשָׂה טֹוב עִם to shew kindness to some one, Gen 26:29. טֹוב לְ to be kind towards some one, Lam 3:25. טֹוב עַיִן a man of a kind eye, i.e. merciful, opp. to רַע עַיִן, Pro 22:9, where see LXX. Vulg. לֹא טֹוב by λιτότης, wicked, evil, Pro 18:5 20:23 .-Often used-(aa) of a good, i.e. a happy lot, compare No. 4. הָיָה טֹוב לִי “it is well with me,” Ecc 8:12, 13 . לָהֶם, לְטֹוב לָנוּ that it may be well with us, or with them, Deu 6:24 10:13 Jer 32:39 (properly for לִהְיֹות טֹוב לְ, as in בְּצַר לִי, לְרַע לָהֶם). טֹוב לִי well for me! Psa 119:17 Lam 3:27. לְטֹוב in a good sense, for welfare (compare טֹובָה), often in medial phrases, Psal. 119:122 Deu 30:9.-(bb) טֹוב בְּעֵינֵי פ׳ good in the eyes of some one, what pleases some one, Num 24:1 Deu 6:18. Gen 16:6, עֲשִׂי־לָהּ הַטֹּוב בְּעֵינַיִךְ “do to her that which seems good to thee” = do with her as thou wilt, Gen 19:8 Jud. 10:15 19:24 also followed by לִפְנֵי Ecc 2:26 and לְ Job 10:3 [but see verb]; Deu 23:17.-Adv. well, very good, 2Sa 3:13 Rth 3:13, and subst. something good, that which is good, Job 7:7 placed as a genitive after a noun, as בִּרְכַּת טֹוב a blessing of good, for a good blessing, Pro 24:25.

(2) goodly, fair, beautiful, used of persons, Exo 2:2 Gen 6:2 and of things, Isa 5:9 more often with the addition of מַרְאֶה Gen 24:16 Est 1:11 2:3 , .

(3) pleasant, agreeable, Gen 3:6 Son 1:2 7:10 . Especially used of smell, שֶׁמֶן הַטֹּוב sweet smelling ointment, Psa 133:2 Isa 39:2 Son 1:3. קָנֶה הַטֹּוב sweet calamus, Jer 6:20.

(4) well off, prosperous, happy, comp. No. 1, (aa); Isa 3:10 Jer 44:17. Psa 112:5, טֹוב־אִישׁ “O happy man.” Ecc 5:4, 17 7:18 compare Lam 3:26 Amo 6:2. (Syr. ܛܘܽܒܰܝܗܘܽܢ ܕ O the blessings of.-Often used for Heb. אַשְׁרֵי, see Mat 5:2, Pesh.)

(5) distinguished, great, excelling. Psa 69:17, כִּי טֹוב חַסְדֶּךָ “for great is thy loving-kindness.” Psa 109:21 (comp. Rth 3:10). Syr. ܛܳܒ adv. very.

(6) cheerful, merry, Est 8:17 1Ki 8:66 בְּלֵב טֹוב with a merry heart. Ecc 9:7.

(7) [Tob], pr.n. of a region beyond Jordan, Jud. 11:3 2Sa 10:6 apparently, i.q. Τούβιον, LXX. Vat. Τώβιον, 1Ma_5:13 .
